rationale: My round-1 REJECT is remediated in the spec text itself. (a) ADDRESSED: R3b persists codex raw stdout as a tracked .codex.log (wrapper-written, persist-before-parse, own falsified clause + AC grep + Manual-proof bullet); its framing correctly extends ADR-0037 §8's plain-reviewable-files/no-signing boundary rather than overclaiming tamper-proofing — a codex slot is now MORE auditable than a claude slot, whose verdict has no source transcript at all. (b) ADDRESSED: the R3 ladder (same-reviewer re-serialize exactly once from its own log / fail CLOSED to MISSING / substitution reserved for a no-verdict quota wall) plus R4's rendered-content precondition, with BOTH falsified clauses naming the rendered-REJECT-replacement path — the laundering route I attacked no longer exists in spec text. (c) ADDRESSED: sha? advisory-only with its falsified clause. Re-attack: chain-of-custody, re-prompt-deviation, partial-render boundary, and ALLOWED_CLI-indirection all resolve to either §8's explicitly accepted anti-footgun boundary or behavior-anchored falsified clauses ('runs or references', 'no verdict content was ever rendered') a bead reviewer can falsify with the persisted log as evidence; the authoritative in-binary gate bounds residual harm. Remaining risks are implementation-time-checkable. APPROVE.
findings: [R3-a audit trail] ADDRESSED. R3b requires every codex wrapper to persist unmodified stdout to <spec-dir>/reviews/<slug>/<slot>-round-<N>.codex.log; the R3 bullet sequences persist BEFORE parse (so the log exists regardless of outcome, including the quota case — making the substitution precondition auditable); falsified clause + AC grep + Manual-proof bullet all present. The §8 framing is honest: the touchpoint reasserts 'no signing … unchanged' immediately before claiming auditability, so 'auditable against its source' reads as §8-sense post-hoc reviewability, not tamper-proofing.

[R3-b anti-laundering, was HIGHEST severity] ADDRESSED. Three-step ladder: (1) rendered-but-malformed → same reviewer, exactly once, fed its own .codex.log, re-serialize without re-reviewing, same slot + family reviewer_id; (2) still unparseable → MISSING, no file, gate Blocks, never substituted; (3) substitution EXCLUSIVELY for a quota wall with no verdict content rendered, and only when claude_sub_on_quota==true. R3's falsified clause names the exact path I attacked ('any path … by which a rendered REJECT/REQUEST_CHANGES … replaced by a different reviewer's verdict or by a re-reviewed APPROVE'); R4's names the trigger ('triggered by a parse failure on a rendered verdict'). Open Questions and Manual proof are aligned end-to-end.

[R3-c sha? semantics] ADDRESSED. R1: advisory-only display hint, reviewed_head_sha self-resolved by panel create from --target, works when omitted; falsified clause 'if sha? (rather than panel create) sets the recorded reviewed_head_sha'.

[RE-ATTACK 1: chain of custody terminates at the wrapper] HELD — not a spec defect. True that a wrapper fabricating a verdict could fabricate the log, but ADR-0037 §8 explicitly declares every gate input agent-writable, forbids signing/hashing at this layer, and relies on git fingerprints as the deterrent; the .codex.log adds a second artifact a forger must consistently fabricate, strictly increasing §8-style auditability. R3b is consistent with the boundary, not an overclaim.

[RE-ATTACK 2: re-prompt deviation] HELD with non-blocking nit. For a codex slot the step-1 re-prompt is a fresh codex invocation fed the persisted .codex.log; a second invocation could in principle deviate, but the spec binds what text can bind (feed back own output, 'without re-reviewing', identity pinned) and the falsified clause makes a re-reviewed APPROVE a spec violation checkable at bead review against the re-prompt construction; runtime disobedience is detectable by diffing the re-emitted verdict against the persisted log. Nit for plan/impl: the Manual-proof bullet checks reviewer_id identity but could also assert the re-emitted verdict VALUE matches the verdict expressed in the .codex.log.

[RE-ATTACK 3: partial-render boundary] HELD. Step 1 defines 'rendered' as 'produced verdict content but it is not valid schema JSON' — a REJECT-in-progress truncated by the quota wall fits, so it routes to the ladder, not substitution; step 3/R4 require 'no verdict content was ever rendered' / 'an empty-handed slot, not a rendered-but-malformed one'. Because stdout persistence precedes parsing, the classification is auditable post-hoc: a .codex.log containing half a REJECT next to a claude-sub verdict falsifies R4 at bead review. Residual definitional gray zone ('verdict content' vs pre-verdict preamble) is small — preamble carries no verdict to launder — and log-covered.

[RE-ATTACK 4: ALLOWED_CLI indirection] HELD. The AC greps are lexical proxies — 'mindspec '+dynamic-verb concatenation would pass every grep — but R5's falsified clause is behavioral, not lexical: 'runs or references mindspec complete or any lifecycle-mutating verb' and 'if the ALLOWED_CLI allowlist admits any command beyond the four named', plus prose requiring the workflow to 'constrain its agent steps' to the allowlist; an aliased/constructed invocation still runs the verb and falsifies R5 at bead review. Harm ceiling is bounded regardless: the authoritative in-binary gate inside mindspec complete (ADR-0037 spec-099/102 amendments) blocks a premature complete whoever invokes it — the workflow was never the enforcement point. Nit: R5's falsified clause could explicitly name dynamically-constructed/aliased commands.

[Deviations] A (new requirement numbered 3b, no renumbering): cosmetic, keeps cross-round references stable — fine. B (literal-nowhere + ALLOWED_CLI, stricter than the ask): accepted; the allowlist declaration satisfying the positive greps is by design since the declaration IS the pinned artifact; the lexical-proxy caveat is covered by the behavioral falsified clause. C (research doc decoupled, not committed): sound from the gate-integrity lens — removes an unverifiable citation without load-bearing loss.
